title: In Census, 'stable' live-in couple to be counted as married
description: Census 2027 will recognize live-in couples in stable unions as married.  The self-enumeration portal clarifies that such relationships should be treated as married. This marks a public acknowledgment, though officials note similar declarations were accepted in past censuses.  The question is part of the houselisting operations.

Wadettiwar expressed fears for Kharat's safety in custody and demanded action against MLAs who visited him, while Bawankule urged evidence submission to agencies instead of media claims.

title: Bengali actor Rahul Arunoday Banerjee dies while shooting for series in Odisha
description: In a heartbreaking turn of events, the talented Bengali actor Rahul Arunoday Banerjee lost his life at just 43 while shooting in Odisha. It was reported that he tragically drowned after persuading the crew to capture an unscripted shot with a drone in what seemed a harmless knee-deep water.

This breach, despite denied requests, has prompted an official inquiry. The Pakistan Cricket Board faces a disciplinary decision, with potential fines or bans, as the tournament grapples with heightened security and fuel shortage concerns.

MI openers Rickelton and Rohit Sharma powered a record chase, securing a six-wicket win against Kolkata Knight Riders after KKR posted 220.

Bengaluru man kills mother, ailing sister, injures minor over 'mounting debt'
description: A 26-year-old man allegedly attacked his family, resulting in two deaths, due to mounting business debts and his sister's critical illness. He then attempted suicide. The incident, which occurred in Mallenahalli, saw his mother and sister die on the spot, while he and his nephew remain in critical condition.

title: BJP is the ‘A team’ of Kerala, says Modi at campaign meet
description: Positioning the BJP-led NDA as a decisive alternative, the Prime Minister said that an NDA government would advance the vision of a “Viksit Kerala”

title: Mojtaba Khamenei lauds Iraq's support in new message, remains out of public eye
description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the war on February 28, has yet to make a public appearance since assuming the role. So far, he has communicated only through a limited number of written statements. His absence from public view has fuelled speculation about his health and whereabouts. However, state television and some Iranian officials have suggested that he is recovering from injuries sustained in an airstrike.

title: Promises delivered and undelivered during 5 years of DMK government
description: The govt. introduced schemes that did not figure in its manifesto, even while delivering key assurances such as Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam and free bus travel; but it has not formulated the Higher Education policy, set up Kalaignar Canteens or created special courts for corruption cases

title: Govt eyes flexible-fuel vehicles’ faster rollout
description: People aware of the developments said since major car and two-wheeler manufacturers have their prototype FFV models ready, now govt needs to create the ecosystem for adoption of these vehicles. Govt has maintained that 20% ethanol blending in petrol has helped India save imports of around 4.5 crore barrels (700 crore litres) of crude annually.

title: RBI’s forced dollar unwind to keep rupee from sliding, bruise banks
description: Until Friday, banks could run net open positions of up to 25% of their net worth. In practice, large lenders often accumulated sizeable long dollar bets, sometimes in excess of of $1 billion, on expectations of rupee depreciation. The new cap forces a rapid reversal. By April 10, 2026, banks must cut these exposures to $100 million. This compels them to sell dollars and buy rupees to close the gap.

title: No jail term for farm goods, textiles export rule slip-ups
description: Similarly, failure to furnish information or sample, or furnishing false information or sample and damaging books of account or refusal to furnish it will not result in a jail term of up to three months, instead a fine of Rs 10,000-25,000 can be levied under the Handlooms (Reservation of Articles for Production) Act.

title: 31 arrested for Ram Navami clashes in Bengal district
description: Police say 31 people have been arrested and 21 injured in the clashes; prohibitory orders have been imposed

title: Foreign brokerages cut India Inc’s earnings, index targets
description: Foreign broking major Goldman Sachs has cut the Nifty target to 25,900 points from 29,300 points earlier while Citigroup has revised down their target for the index to 27,000 points from 28,500 points earlier. On its part, HSBC said that historical trend shows that a 20% rise in oil price could drag down earnings of India Inc by 1.3 percentage points. Since the war started crude oil prices are up about 50-55%.

description: As India assumes the IONS chairmanship for the 2026–2028 cycle after a gap of 16 years, IMEX TTX 2026 marks a key milestone in strengthening regional maritime leadership. Conducted in a sophisticated, simulated environment, the exercise addressed complex maritime security challenges in IOR, a region of critical importance to global trade, energy flows and connectivity.

title: Wildlife actively use underpasses on Delhi-Doon economic corridor: Study
description: As per the recently published study by Dehradun-based Wildlife Institute of India, 150 camera traps (CTs) were systematically deployed along underpasses within a 3.5-km stretch — from Mohand village to Ganeshpur in Uttarakhand — and each unit was installed in a manner to ensure detection of a broad spectrum of terrestrial fauna, ranging from small to large-bodied species.

title: Nearly 3 lakh PNG connections added in March: Govt
description: Petroleum secretary Neeraj Mittal, in an online post, said that by giving up their LPG connections, people have helped those who don’t have access to gas cylinders. “Join this strong, bold group of do-good citizens who have come forward to give up LPG to help those get LPG who don’t have PNG,” he said on X.

title: TVK chief Vijay to make electoral debut from Perambur, Tiruchi East constituencies in Tamil Nadu Assembly election
description: TVK general secretary ‘Bussy’ N. Anand will contest from T. Nagar in Chennai, while K.A. Sengottaiyan will test the waters from his home turf, Gobichettipalayam. Aadhav Arjuna will contest from Villivakkam, K.G. Arunraaj from Tiruchengode and C.T.R. Nirmal Kumar from Thirupparankundram.

title: India accounts for 1 in 10 global maternal deaths: Lancet report
description: India’s long-term numbers reflect both progress and persistent gaps. Maternal deaths fell sharply from about 1.19 lakh in 1990 to 36,900 in 2015 and further to 24,700 in 2023. And the maternal mortality ratio declined from 508 in 1990 to 116 per one lakh live births in 2023, indicating substantial gains but also unfinished work.

title: Tea body seeks clarity on Assam land transfer policy for plantation workers
description: Two months ahead of the elections, the BJP-led government amended a law to grant legal land rights to 3.5 lakh families of plantation workers living in the labour lines of more than 800 tea estates

Jahima Khatun’s name was deleted because her father’s name was misspelt in one document. EC released a fourth supplementary list Sunday with around two lakh names from among the close to 60 lakh that were under judicial adjudication. Of the 40 lakh cases disposed of by judicial officers, around 18 lakh have been rejected.

BJP drops 'Greater Kashmir' charge, backs J&K reorganisation bill
description: The proposal by PDP legislator Waheed Parra seeks 16 new districts across J&K and two additional divisions in Jammu. J&K currently has two divisions — Jammu and Kashmir — with 10 districts each. BJP said the bill should be tabled for debate, flagging objections only to names “Pir Panjal” and “Chenab Valley” for proposed Rajouri–Poonch and Doda–Kishtwar–Ramban divisions.

title: Transport ministry proposes secured cover for moving dumpers, tippers
description: The draft notification also proposes that devices such as rear-view mirrors, indirect vision systems, guard rails, direction indicator rub-rails (rubber beading up to 20 mm on each side), side footsteps, and the load body enclosure system for tippers and dumpers should not be included while calculating the overall height. TOI on January 24 had reported about the govt’s move.

title: Rahul Arunoday Banerjee’s last video from set goes viral
description: A video of Bengali actor Rahul Arunoday Banerjee from the sets of his show Bholebaba Paar Karega is circulating on social media following news of his untimely death at 43. Rahul reportedly drowned at Talsari beach in Odisha during the shoot. The now-viral clip shows Rahul preparing for a shot with a female co-actor near a car, surrounded by a crowd of onlookers.

title: IPL 2026: Why can’t Cameron Green bowl yet? Ask Cricket Australia, says KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane after MI defeat
Scott McLaughlin escapes huge IndyCar crash at 170mph in live race
description: Scott McLaughlin survived a massive crash during an IndyCar practice session at Barber Motorsports Park. His car hit the barrier at nearly 170mph and broke through fencing before stopping near trees. Safety teams acted quickly, and he walked away without serious injury. He later returned to qualify, finishing 14th, making it a shocking yet relieving day for everyone watching.

title: IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma, Rickelton fire MI to record chase, ending 13-year jinx
description: Mumbai Indians shattered their opening match losing streak, chasing down KKR's 220 with a six-wicket victory. Rickelton and Rohit Sharma's explosive 148-run stand powered the chase. Despite KKR's strong batting performance, including fifties from Rahane and Raghuvanshi, MI's bowlers, led by Shardul Thakur, managed to restrict them. This win marks a significant start for MI in IPL 2026.

A shadow war? The Shia-Sunni equation behind Iran's Gulf attacks
description: For decades, West Asia had learned to live with tension. Proxy battles, covert strikes, deniable operations and quiet escalations had defined the region’s uneasy equilibrium. But what unfolded in recent weeks broke that pattern. Iran did not just respond to the joint strikes by the United States and Israel, it widened the battlefield. In doing so, it dragged the Gulf into a conflict many believed would remain contained between Tehran, Washington and Tel Aviv. The shock was not limited to the region.

Google ex-CEO Eric Schmidt says coding is no longer a key skill
description: 0Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt says the best programmers no longer write code—they write specs, set evaluation criteria, and let AI work overnight. Schmidt described a startup programmer whose AI finishes by 4 AM what would have taken him six months and 10 engineers at Google. He argued top programmers will become more valuable, not less, and that AI's biggest impact will be automating costly corporate operations like billing, accounting, and logistics.

title: Hailee Steinfeld shares emotional note before first child with Josh Allen
description: Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld are nearing the arrival of their first child, adding a personal dimension to the quarterback’s upcoming season. Steinfeld’s recent newsletter offers a candid glimpse into her life, from honoring her mother to navigating late pregnancy moments. Alongside this, her film Sinners enjoyed award success, with Michael B. Jordan acknowledging her during his Oscar speech. Together, these moments reflect a transition period where career highlights and personal milestones are unfolding side by side.

title: Baker Mayfield’s Bucs linked to $118M Commanders linebacker deal
description: Following Lavonte David’s retirement annoucement, The Athletic suggests the Tampa Bay Buccaneers target veteran linebacker Bobby Wagner to fill the legendary gap. Wagner is a Super Bowl champion with extensive accolades to his name. He has a high football IQ. He possesses elite run-stopping and pass-rushing abilities. This could potentially fit into Tampa Bay’s needs.
